purpose: Defines the !contracts slash command — read-only audit of API contracts between frontend and backend. Greps frontend for fetch/axios/useSWR/useQuery patterns to extract method+endpoint+request shape+expected response, then greps backend for route definitions (Express/Hono/Fastify/Next.js API routes/Firebase onRequest|onCall) to extract handler shapes. Cross-matches each frontend call to its backend handler, comparing request shapes, response shapes, auth requirements, and error handling. Flags orphans (backend with no caller, frontend calls to non-existent endpoints) and drift (key casing, missing fields). Output is a contract-status table plus mismatch details and recommendations. Master runs serially.
gotchas: Check actual code, not documentation — documentation lies, code is truth.
Auth/middleware context is mandatory in flagging: an admin-only endpoint where the frontend doesn't send admin tokens must be flagged even if shapes match.
Both directions of orphan checking: backend endpoints with no frontend caller (might be external API) AND frontend calls to non-existent endpoints (broken features waiting to happen).
Key-casing mismatches (avatarUrl vs avatar_url) are real contract drift, not cosmetic — surface them.
patterns: Bidirectional matching: frontend calls ↔ backend handlers → compare each pair.
Five-axis comparison: endpoint exists, request shape, response shape, auth, error handling.
